title: Selecting the Right Speedup Dial
concept: Use quantization to reduce memory footprint; use KV caching and speculative decoding to minimize generation latency.

exercises.prompt: How do quantization, KV caching, and speculative decoding differ in how they help AI models?

options.text: Quantization saves memory; KV caching and speculative decoding save generation time.
They all do the exact same thing in the same way.
None of these methods help with speed or memory.

exercises.explain: Quantization reduces the memory footprint, while KV caching and speculative decoding are designed to minimize generation latency (the time it takes to produce new tokens).

cards.title: Smarter, Faster, Lighter
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how massive AI brains can run on tiny devices, or how they spit out answers so quickly? It's not magic—it's all about turning the right performance dials to save memory and speed up word generation!

cards.title: Meet the Three Speedup Dials
cards.body:
To speed up AI models, we use three main dials:
• Quantization: Shrinking the size of the model's numbers to save memory.
• KV Caching: Storing past conversations in memory so the AI doesn't have to re-read everything for every new word.
• Speculative Decoding: Using a tiny, fast AI to draft words, and a big AI to quickly double-check and approve them.

cards.title: The Kitchen Speedup Dial
cards.body: Imagine running a fast-food kitchen. Quantization is like using pre-chopped ingredients to save fridge space. KV caching is keeping a prep table with cooked ingredients ready so you don't start from scratch for every customer. Speculative decoding is like having a helper guess the customer's order and prepare it ahead of time; if they guess right, the food is served instantly!

cards.title: Putting it to Work
cards.body: If you try to run a massive 70-billion parameter model on a single gaming computer, it will crash because of low memory. By applying 4-bit quantization, you shrink its memory footprint so it fits. Then, by enabling KV caching, you make it generate sentences smoothly instead of slowing down with every word.

cards.title: The Speed vs. Size Trap
cards.body: People often think that to make an AI generate text faster, you just need to shrink its memory size. But shrinking memory (quantization) only helps it fit on the device. To actually speed up the word-by-word generation, you need tools like KV caching and speculative decoding!

cards.title: Memory vs. Speed
cards.body: Depending on your bottleneck (lack of memory vs. slow word generation), you need to choose the right optimization dial.

right.text: Use KV Caching and Speculative Decoding to generate words without lagging.

left.text: Use Quantization to shrink weights from 16-bit to 4-bit, saving precious RAM space.

exercises.prompt: Which technique is specifically designed to shrink an AI's memory footprint by using less precise numbers?
exercises.explain: Quantization works by reducing the precision of the numbers (weights) used by the AI, which directly shrinks how much RAM/memory the model requires.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Quantization is the only technique you need to guarantee super-fast word generation speed.
exercises.explain: Quantization is used to reduce the memory footprint. To make the model generate words faster, techniques like KV caching and speculative decoding are required.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of generation speedups.
exercises.sentence: To avoid recalculating past context over and over during generation, we use ________.
exercises.explain: KV caching stores previous calculations so the model does not have to rebuild its memory of the entire chat history for every single new word.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes Speculative Decoding?

options.text: An AI that translates text between three languages simultaneously.
A small draft model suggesting words that a larger model verifies.
An AI that shrinks its files by rounding numbers to the nearest whole integer.

exercises.explain: Speculative decoding uses a smaller, faster "draft" model to guess words, which the main model quickly approves or rejects, speeding up the generation process.

exercises.prompt: You want to deploy a large chatbot on a smartphone. The phone has very limited RAM (memory), and users hate waiting for each word to appear. Which combination of speedup dials should you turn?

options.text: Quantization only, because smaller files automatically generate text much faster.
Speculative decoding only, as memory footprint has no relation to hardware limits.
Quantization for the memory limit, and KV caching with speculative decoding for generation speed.
None of these; you must always train a completely new model from scratch to fit a phone.

exercises.explain: Quantization shrinks the model size (saving memory), while KV caching (avoiding recomputing past context) and speculative decoding (using a tiny model to guess draft tokens) specifically reduce the time it takes to generate each new word.
